export default function MDXContent({

  return <MDXLayout {...layoutProps} {...props} components={components} mdxType="MDXLayout">



    <h2>{`Warp-Speed`}</h2>
    <WarpSpeedCanvas mdxType="WarpSpeedCanvas" />
    <h4>{`(no, it's not a gif)`}</h4>
    <p>
  <a href="https://github.com/SmithKy3/warp-speed" target="_blank">
    Repo / code here <CodeSlash mdxType="CodeSlash" />
  </a>
    </p>
    <p>
  <a href="https://www.npmjs.com/package/warpspeed" target="_blank">
    NPM page here <Npm mdxType="Npm" />
  </a>
    </p>
    <p>{`This is an NPM package I wrote during the first COVID-19 lockdown in 2020 and it served two purposes;
introduce me to publishing to NPM and to provide me with a break from my`}</p>
    <p>
  <a href="/my-work/space-thing">space-thing</a> project that I was also
  building out at the time.
    </p>
    <p>{`I was working through a lot of Vector algebra with HTML5 canvas animations and was figuring out
how best to give the feel of depth on a screen / web page. I decided I could probably remake the star wars
acceleration to hyperspace animation pretty easily. So, I knocked this out over the course of a day or so.
It’s since had a couple of minor upgrades but the overarching algorithm is much the same.`}</p>
    <p>{`For each "star", I'm doing the following:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Generate a random point in 3D space`}
        <ul parentName="li">
          <li parentName="ul">{`Clamp X between 0 and half of the canvas width`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul">{`Clamp Y between 0 and half of the canvas height`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul">{`Z needs to be clamped between 0 and any number really, but I found using the max X works well`}</li>
        </ul>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Every frame / every time you redraw the star:`}
        <ul parentName="li">
          <li parentName="ul">{`Instead of using just X and Y as the center of the star on the canvas, multiply both by (MAX_Z / Z)`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul">{`Decrement Z`}</li>
        </ul>
      </li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`As Z moves approaches zero, the aforementioned coefficient (MAX_Z / Z) approaches infinity, meaning X and Y increase
and move towards the edge of the screen.`}</p>
    <p>{`Then, once Z goes below zero, I "recycle" the star and give it regenerated coordinates.`}</p>

    </MDXLayout>;
